Test Scenario: AWS credentials are stored securely and not exposed in UI or logs
Acceptance Criteria: Credentials are never displayed in plain text; secret key masked; no credential leakage in browser console or logs
Steps to Execute: 1. Configure AWS credentials in SAELAR; 2. Inspect page source and browser console; 3. Check that only masked/placeholder values visible
Expected Result: No credential exposure in any client-side context

Test Scenario: Application enforces secure session behavior
Acceptance Criteria: Session does not persist sensitive data in URL parameters; logout clears credentials from session
Steps to Execute: 1. Log in with credentials; 2. Check URL for any credential strings; 3. Log out and verify session cleared
Expected Result: No credentials in URL; clean logout

Test Scenario: Application supports HTTPS when configured
Acceptance Criteria: When SSL certs present
Steps to Execute: app serves over HTTPS; no mixed content warnings
Expected Result: 1. Configure SSL; 2. Access via https://; 3. Verify padlock and no mixed-content errors

Test Scenario: Air-gapped mode operates without external network
Acceptance Criteria: When SAELAR_AIRGAPPED=true and Ollama running
Steps to Execute: all features work with no outbound AWS/internet calls
Expected Result: 1. Set SAELAR_AIRGAPPED=true; 2. Disconnect or block outbound; 3. Run assessment and AI features

Test Scenario: NIST 800-53 Rev 5 controls are accurately represented
Acceptance Criteria: All 13 cloud-relevant control families assessed; control IDs and descriptions align with NIST Rev 5
Steps to Execute: 1. Run NIST Assessment; 2. Review control list; 3. Cross-reference sample controls to NIST 800-53 Rev 5

Test Scenario: Assessment queries correct AWS services
Acceptance Criteria: Findings reference actual AWS resources; IAM/S3/EC2/CloudTrail/Config etc. queried appropriately
Steps to Execute: 1. Run cloud assessment; 2. Verify findings map to real AWS API responses; 3. Spot-check IAM/EC2/S3 findings

Test Scenario: Security Hub findings map to NIST controls
Acceptance Criteria: Security Hub findings imported and correctly mapped to NIST 800-53 control families
Steps to Execute: 1. Enable Security Hub; 2. Run assessment; 3. View Security Hub findings tab; 4. Verify NIST mapping

Test Scenario: Risk scores follow NIST SP 800-30 methodology
Acceptance Criteria: Risk matrix (Likelihood x Impact) produces severity levels; CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW thresholds correct
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment with mix of findings; 2. Review risk scores; 3. Verify escalation rules (e.g. 2+ CRITICAL = Overall CRITICAL)

Test Scenario: CISA KEV findings are identified
Acceptance Criteria: BOD 22-01 tab shows KEV-listed vulnerabilities when present; remediation timeline guidance provided
Steps to Execute: 1. Import or simulate KEV findings; 2. Check BOD 22-01 tab; 3. Verify KEV identification

Test Scenario: User can navigate between all major tabs within 3 clicks
Acceptance Criteria: NIST Assessment/AWS Console/Chad/Risk Calculator/SSP Generator/BOD 22-01/Threat Modeling/Artifacts accessible
Steps to Execute: 1. Start from home; 2. Navigate to each tab; 3. Count clicks required

Test Scenario: AWS credential configuration is intuitive
Acceptance Criteria: Credential screen has clear fields; validation provides actionable error messages; successful config shows confirmation
Steps to Execute: 1. Open credential config; 2. Enter invalid then valid creds; 3. Verify validation feedback

Test Scenario: Dashboard metrics and charts are legible and meaningful
Acceptance Criteria: Metrics show pass/fail counts; charts render correctly; color coding is consistent
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. View dashboard; 3. Check charts and metrics

Test Scenario: SSP and POA&M generation requires minimal user input
Acceptance Criteria: User selects options and clicks Generate; document downloads without excessive form filling
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Open SSP Generator; 3. Generate SSP and POA&M with minimal input

Test Scenario: Results exportable in usable formats
Acceptance Criteria: CSV/Excel/PDF exports available where applicable; exported data is complete and structured
Steps to Execute: 1. Generate assessment; 2. Export in each format; 3. Open and verify content

Test Scenario: NIST cloud assessment runs to completion
Acceptance Criteria: Full assessment completes without fatal errors; results displayed; all control families processed
Steps to Execute: 1. Configure valid AWS credentials; 2. Run NIST Assessment; 3. Wait for completion

Test Scenario: Chad AI assistant responds to security questions
Acceptance Criteria: Chad provides relevant answers; can generate charts and remediation guidance; graceful fallback if Bedrock unavailable
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Chad tab; 2. Ask security question; 3. Request chart or remediation

Test Scenario: Documents can be uploaded to S3 when configured
Acceptance Criteria: S3 bucket selectable; upload succeeds; documents listable and downloadable
Steps to Execute: 1. Configure S3 bucket; 2. Upload SSP/POA&M; 3. List and download

Test Scenario: Threat modeling module displays control-to-threat mapping
Acceptance Criteria: MITRE ATT&CK mapping visible; asset-threat relationships shown; exportable
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Open Threat Modeling; 3. Review mappings

Test Scenario: Artifacts/evidence can be managed
Acceptance Criteria: Upload download and organize assessment artifacts
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Artifacts tab; 2. Upload file; 3. List and download

Test Scenario: Cloud assessment completes within reasonable time
Acceptance Criteria: Full NIST assessment completes in under 10 minutes for typical account
Steps to Execute: 1. Run full assessment; 2. Record start and end time

Test Scenario: Initial page load responsive
Acceptance Criteria: Application loads within 15 seconds on standard connection
Steps to Execute: 1. Clear cache; 2. Load app; 3. Measure time to interactive

Test Scenario: Assessment history and actions are traceable
Acceptance Criteria: Assessment runs and key actions produce auditable records where applicable
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Review any logs or history; 3. Verify traceability

Test Scenario: Output aligns with FedRAMP documentation expectations
Acceptance Criteria: SSP and POA&M structure suitable for FedRAMP packages
Steps to Execute: 1. Generate SSP and POA&M; 2. Review against FedRAMP template expectations
